title: An interactive platform for geospatial learning
abstract: The invention provides a method for providing a user interactive platform for geospatial learning with respect to a location. The method includes capturing information with respect to the location in the info-graphics, audio, a video and/or a combination thereof. Upon capturing the information, it is stored in a database. The method further includes representing the location through virtual reality, allowing the user to remotely access the location and enabling the user to navigate through the location to retrieve the information captured in the first step.
claims:
Claims I Claim: 1 . A method for providing a user interactive platform for geospatial learning with respect to a location, the method comprising: capturing information with respect to the location in the form of a latitude and longitude, a text, an image, an info-graphics, an audio, a video and/or a combination thereof; storing the information in a database; representing the location through virtual reality; allowing the user to remotely access the location; and enabling the user to navigate through the location to retrieve the information captured in the first step. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the location is at least one selected from a list comprising of an ancient site, a temple, a museum, a statue and an exhibition centre. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the info-graphics comprises of a text, a 3D model of a premise, a manuscript image, a haptic image, a 2D video, a 3D video, and a 3D model. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the audio information comprises of a binaural recording selected from a list comprising of an ambient sound, a vedic chant, a pooja sound, a music, a vocal rendition of classical music, an instrumental folk music, a speech of a person who has a relation to the location, an audio narrative of the location and a pre-gramophone record. 5. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the video information comprises of 360 degree videos and pictures obtained using at least one of a photogrammetry, a 3D scanning and a meshing. 6. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the user is provided an option to view the information in a time dependent manner, wherein the time-dependency is determined by a day cycle, a lunar cycle or a seasonal cycle. 7. An interactive platform for geospatial learning with respect to a location, the platform comprising: a database for retrievably storing information with respect to the location in the form of a latitude and longitude, a text, an image, an info-graphics, an audio, a video and/or a combination thereof; a user interface coupled to the database; and a virtual reality headset operatively coupled to the user interface. wherein the user interface enables the user to navigate through the location to retrieve the information captured in the first step. 8. The platform as claimed in claim 7, wherein the database is a remotely accessible database. 9. The platform as claimed in claim 7, wherein the location is at least one selected from a list comprising of an ancient site, a temple, a museum, a statue and an exhibition centre. 10. The platform as claimed in claim 7, wherein the info graphics comprises of a text, a 3D model of a premise, a 

 manuscript image, a haptic image, a 2D video, a 3D video, and a 3D model. 1 1. The platform as claimed in claim 7, wherein the audio information comprises of a binaural recording selected from a list comprising of an ambient sound, a vedic chant, a pooja sound, a music, a vocal rendition of classical music, an instrumental folk music, a speech of a person who has a relation to the location, an audio narrative of the location and a pre-Gramophone record. 12. The platform as claimed in claim 7, wherein the video information comprises of a 360 degree videos and pictures obtained using photogrammetry, 3D scanning, and meshing. 13. The platform as claimed in claim 7, wherein the user is provided an option to view the information in a time dependent manner, wherein the time-dependency is determined by a day cycle, lunar cycle or seasonal cycle.
